title: Dynatrace Agentic Workflows: Approvals, Agents, and Automation
description:
Explore how SREs, platform engineers, and DevOps teams can move beyond simple notifications and build human‑in‑the‑loop agentic operations using Dynatrace Workflows. In this Observability Lab, Andi and Sonja walk through real-world automation patterns that combine Dynatrace Intelligence, DQL, approvals, and sub‑workflows to operationalize observability data.
You’ll learn how Dynatrace Workflows evolve from basic alerting into powerful orchestration and remediation pipelines, including agentic troubleshooting for Kubernetes environments.

In this session, you’ll learn how to:
- Understand workflow triggers: problem, event, schedule, and on‑demand
- Design simple vs. standard workflows and manage licensing impact
- Use DQL tasks and Jinja templating to enrich workflow context
- Implement approval requests for safe human‑in‑the‑loop automation
- Build reusable sub‑workflows and orchestrators
- Leverage Dynatrace Intelligence (preview) for agentic troubleshooting
- Automate Kubernetes remediation with governance and guardrails
- Observe, audit, and optimize workflows using system events in Grail

Whether you're automating incident response, reporting, governance, or remediation, this video shows how Dynatrace Workflows help teams act on observability data with confidence and control.

title: Build a Dynatrace App with AI in Minutes
description:
Explore how observability practitioners can build custom Dynatrace Apps beyond dashboards using AI coding agents and the Dynatrace App Toolkit. In this Observability Lab, Andi Grabner and Dani Coll walk through a hands-on example that turns Dynatrace data into a domain-specific application for detecting suspicious trades.
You’ll see how logs and business events ingested into Dynatrace can be transformed into an interactive app—without being a TypeScript or React expert—by combining AI agents, DQL, and the Dynatrace design system.
In this video, you’ll learn how to:
- Build a Dynatrace App from scratch using the App Toolkit
- Use AI coding agents to generate UI and backend logic
- Query business events with Dynatrace Query Language (DQL)
- Leverage Strato components for consistent UI design
- Run apps locally and understand the deployment model
- Use MCP servers to validate DQL queries and UI components
- Extend observability beyond dashboards with domain knowledge

This session is ideal for SREs, DevOps engineers, platform teams, and Dynatrace administrators who want to prototype custom apps, validate ideas quickly, and deliver real operational value—without heavy development overhead.

🔗 Useful links
First app in 5 minutes: https://dt-url.net/fo03826
AI for app development: https://dt-url.net/i423826
Dynatrace MCP Server: https://dt-url.net/mcp
VSCode and Cursor Extensions: https://dt-url.net/1x638dt
Inside Dynatrace Apps Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLqt2rd0eew1bu5vGLUJ3pr2SgNpnpKb71

title: Dynatrace Can Do THAT with OpenTelemetry | Host Monitoring
description:
Explore how SREs, platform engineers, and infrastructure teams can monitor hosts using OpenTelemetry-native tooling and visualize everything in Dynatrace. Join Adriana Villela and Mikko Viitanen (Product Manager, Dynatrace) as they walk through the new OTel Host Monitoring extension, showing how standard OpenTelemetry Collectors can deliver host and process observability—without proprietary agents.
In this episode of Dynatrace Can Do THAT with OpenTelemetry, you’ll learn how to:
- Monitor bare‑metal hosts and VMs using standard OpenTelemetry Collectors
- Use optimized host metrics with multiple collection intervals (1 min, 5 min, 1 hour)
- Enrich telemetry with resource detection for correct host correlation
- Visualize host, process, and OS metrics in Dynatrace Infrastructure & Operations
- Correlate host metrics with logs and distributed traces
- Understand permission requirements for advanced disk and I/O metrics
- Enable and activate the OTel Host Monitoring extension in Dynatrace Hub

This solution is pure OpenTelemetry, vendor‑neutral at the Collector level, and designed for teams standardizing on OTel while still gaining deep infrastructure observability in Dynatrace. The feature is currently in early access, making it a great opportunity to experiment and provide feedback.

title: How to query Smartscape on Grail │ Dynatrace DQL
description:
Explore how observability practitioners can use Smartscape on Grail to model, query, and analyze real‑time topology data in Dynatrace. Join Adam Gardner as they walk through how nodes, edges, and signals come together in Grail—and how you can query this data using Dynatrace Query Language (DQL) to answer real operational and security questions.
In this hands‑on walkthrough, you’ll learn how to:
Understand nodes, entities, and signals in Smartscape on Grail
Explore static and dynamic relationships between monitored entities
Query Smartscape topology data in real time using DQL
Traverse relationships to analyze service‑to‑service communication
Investigate Kubernetes metadata, deployments, and namespaces
Assess security risks using cloud configuration data (AWS IAM roles)
Use the Dynatrace Playground to experiment with Smartscape queries
Whether you operate Kubernetes platforms, cloud services, or distributed microservices, this video shows how Smartscape on Grail enables deep architectural insight directly from live telemetry data.

🔗 Useful links
Explore the scenario on the Dynatrace Playground: https://playground.apps.dynatrace.com/ui/apps/dynatrace.notebooks/notebook/ebb02fc2-0d32-4f3b-8b23-3d037ff98466
Learn more about Smartscape on Grail in Dynatrace Docs: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/platform/grail/smartscape-on-grail 
Dynatrace Playground signup: https://dt-url.net/devrel-signup-playground

title: How to Debug Production Services with Dynatrace │ Live Debugger
description:
Dynatrace practitioners often need to debug production issues without stopping a running service. In this episode of How to with Dynatrace, Josh Hendrick walks you through how to use the Dynatrace Live Debugger to capture code‑level insights safely, quickly, and without disruption.
You’ll learn how to choose the exact instances to debug, connect your Git repository, set non‑breaking breakpoints, and inspect captured snapshots — all directly inside Dynatrace.
What you’ll learn
- How to select specific services, instances, pods, or labels to target for debugging
- How to connect source code repositories (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, or local)
- How to authenticate with OAuth or personal access tokens
- How to browse source code and place non‑breaking breakpoints
- How Live Debugger captures snapshots without pausing the application
- How to inspect variables, process metadata, stack traces, Span IDs, and Trace IDs
- How Live Debugger connects to distributed tracing

Dynatrace Live Debugger helps teams safely debug in production by providing real-time code‑level visibility without disrupting users or services.

🔗 Useful links
Get started with the Live Debugger on the Dynatrace University: https://university.dynatrace.com/learn/courses/230/introduction-to-live-debugger 
Visit the Dynatrace Playground: https://dt-url.net/devrel-signup-playground
Watch our deep-dive series on Live Debugger:

title: Scale Multi-Cloud Monitoring with Smartscape and Grail
description:
Explore how cloud operations teams, SREs, platform engineers, and developers can simplify and scale multi‑cloud observability with the Dynatrace Clouds App.
Join Stefan Penner (Principal Product Manager) and Andi Grabner as they walk through the new cloud connection model, Smartscape on Grail, tag enrichment, DQL-powered insights, and automated remediation workflows.

In this hands‑on Observability Labs episode, learn how to:
- Set up AWS, Azure, and GCP connections with the redesigned onboarding workflow
- Monitor multiple cloud regions and accounts with Smartscape and Grail
- Use tag enrichment to drive ownership, cost allocation, and routing
- Analyze cloud resources with DQL and correlate metrics, logs, events, and topology
- Identify underutilized cloud resources and automate optimization with workflows
- Query cloud configuration (IAM roles, AMIs, Lambda layers, VPCs) at scale
- Detect issues with Davis® AI and perform deep analysis through traces, logs, and services
-Use AWS EventBridge health events to trigger automated failover and resiliency workflows

Whether you operate cloud platforms, run production workloads, or manage observability strategy, this session shows how Dynatrace helps teams run resilient, cost‑efficient, and fully observable cloud environments.

title: Set Up Problem Notifications with Simple Workflow Templates
description:
In this Observability Labs session, Andi Grabner welcomes product manager Sonja Chevre to walk through how Dynatrace users can get reliable, targeted notifications when issues occur — and how this fits into the broader journey toward intelligent, automated remediation.

Learn how alerts become Davis® events, how Dynatrace Intelligence correlates them into problems, and how to use workflows to notify teams in Slack, Microsoft Teams, email, or trigger automation. This is part one of a multi‑episode deep dive on building agentic, proactive, and context‑rich workflows.

You’ll see real product demos, best practices, and how to use workflow templates to accelerate setup.

In this video, you’ll learn:
- How alerts, Davis® events, and problems flow through Dynatrace
- How to build problem‑based triggers in workflows
- When to use simple vs. standard workflows
- How to set up Slack, Teams, or email notifications
- Why service users are essential for production workflows
- How workflow templates accelerate configuration
- How to test your workflow execution
- How to inspect workflow runs directly from the Problem App
- When to use Davis event triggers instead of problem triggers
- How this ties into agentic remediation and intelligent automation

Whether you're an SRE, DevOps engineer, or platform team member, this episode shows how to set up reliable, noise‑free alerting paths that scale in cloud‑native environments.

🔗 Useful links
Try this workflow setup in the Dynatrace Playground: https://dt-url.net/devrel-signup-playground
Check out the Dynatrace Intelligence App: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/dynatrace-intelligence/dynatrace-intelligence-integrations/copilot-for-workflows
Explore workflow triggers and system events: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/analyze-explore-automate/workflows
Get help or share questions with the community: https://dynatr.ac/3M0AzM6

title: Right‑Size Kubernetes CPU and Memory with Dynatrace Smartscape
description:
Kubernetes overprovisioning is one of the biggest sources of cloud waste. In this Observe & Resolve episode, learn how to automatically right‑size Kubernetes workloads using Dynatrace Smartscape, DQL, and workflow automation.

Henrik walks you through a production-ready workflow that detects underutilized workloads, calculates safe resource recommendations, and creates pull requests that optimize your manifests — all without manual intervention.

You’ll learn how to:
- Detect overprovisioned Kubernetes workloads at scale
- Query Kubernetes entities using the new Smartscape entity model and DQL
- Access workload, pod, and container manifests stored in Grail
- Calculate safe right‑sizing recommendations using resource utilization data
- Build a Dynatrace workflow that patches manifests and opens GitHub PRs
- Notify workload owners automatically and track improvements
- Reduce cloud spend and improve sustainability without manual review

This workflow is ideal for DevOps, SREs, and platform engineering teams operating Kubernetes at scale.

🔗 Useful links
Try out the demo on GitHub: https://dt-url.net/devrel-gh-k8s-res-opt
Explore the Dynatrace Playground → https://dt-url.net/devrel-signup-playground

title: Shriram Capital enhances security and delivers inclusive financial services with Dynatrace
description:
In this video, Sendil Kumar Venkatesan, CTO of Shriram Capital, shares how the company transformed its digital operations with Dynatrace. By replacing fragmented monitoring tools, Shriram gained full visibility across its hybrid cloud infrastructure supporting the Shriram One app (17+ million customers) and Astra B2B app (30,000 field agents). This unified approach enables proactive issue resolution and saves 15 hours of manpower every week and 20% resource savings.

title: Hands‑on Smartscape | Visualize Dependencies Across Your Entire Stack
description:
Explore how Site Reliability Engineers (SREs), platform teams, and observability practitioners can use the Smartscape App to understand full‑stack context across infrastructure, Kubernetes, cloud services, applications, and business processes. In this session, Andi walks through how Dynatrace visualizes dependencies across all monitored entities — from hosts and processes to containers, pods, services, and user interactions.
In this hands-on walkthrough, you’ll learn how to:

- Navigate the Smartscape App in Dynatrace
- Explore entity relationships across hosts, containers, services, and Kubernetes
- Use isolation views, filter policies, and topology exploration
- Understand how problems map to underlying infrastructure
- Analyze service dependencies with the Service Graph
- View Kubernetes objects and cloud resources in context
- Jump directly to detail apps (Hosts, Services, Kubernetes, etc.)
- Use Smartscape data stored in Grail and accessible through DQL.

Whether you’re troubleshooting incidents, validating deployments, or exploring your topology, Smartscape helps you understand how everything connects across your environment.

title: Dynatrace Intelligence, Smartscape on Grail, Clouds App, and RUM | What’s New in Dynatrace
description:
Discover everything new in Dynatrace as of March 2026 — including major announcements unveiled at Perform 2026. This episode covers the latest advancements across Dynatrace Intelligence, Smartscape on Grail, cloud monitoring, AI observability, and real user monitoring. 

In this video, we walk through:
- Dynatrace Intelligence: Remote MCP Server, Dynatrace Assist (formerly Davis CoPilot), and new agentic operations capabilities
- Extended AI Observability: OpenTelemetry generative AI semantic conventions support and new integrations (Claude, OpenInference)
- Smartscape on Grail: Fully indexed, queryable Smartscape entities with DQL and new domain‑specific views
- Cloud Operations: Redesigned cloud ingestion via the new Clouds app, including AWS (available today), plus Azure and Google Cloud in preview
- Real User Monitoring: Extended SPA support and three new apps—Users & Sessions, Experience Vitals, and Error Inspector
- Agentic Ecosystem: New integrations for AWS DevOps agent, Azure SRE agent, ServiceNow Assist, Atlassian Rovo, PagerDuty, and more

Whether you're operating cloud‑native environments, instrumenting AI workloads, or scaling observability across teams, these updates help you analyze, automate, and take action faster with Dynatrace. title: Why Is My Service Slow? Root Cause Analysis with Dynatrace Distributed Tracing
description:
Stop guessing at performance issues — learn how to diagnose them with confidence. In this hands-on session, Andi Grabner walks you through practical performance debugging techniques using the Dynatrace Services App.

You'll learn how to identify response time anomalies, compare problematic timeframes, drill into slow endpoints, and navigate distributed traces to pinpoint the exact root cause. In this example, we uncover a misbehaving image-resizing lambda causing massive delays.

Follow along on the Dynatrace Playground and replicate each step yourself.

What you'll learn:

- How to spot performance degradations using golden signals
- How to use Response Time analysis and histograms for fast insights
- How to compare service behavior across timeframes
- How to isolate slow endpoints and downstream dependencies
- How to investigate distributed traces to reveal the true root cause
- How Dynatrace Davis AI automatically detects anomalies and links them to code or config changes
- Tips for navigating Dynatrace faster (like Ctrl+K global search)

Whether you're a performance engineer, SRE, developer, or architect, this video gives you step-by-step techniques for hotspot detection, trace analysis, dependency investigation, and automated problem notifications.


🔗 Useful links
Dynatrace Playground: https://dt-url.net/dt-devrel-playground
AppSpotlight Services App: https://dt-url.net/tg030oa

---------------------------—

01:10 – Identifying Response Time Spikes

02:00 – Zooming into Problematic Timeframes
02:20 – Using the Response Time Analysis Tab

title: Hands‑On dtctl: Use GitHub Copilot & AI Agents to Automate Dynatrace
description:
Meet dtctl — the open‑source Dynatrace CLI built to let humans and AI agents (e.g., Copilot) browse, edit, query, and fully automate Dynatrace configurations end‑to‑end. Repo & docs below ↓

In this Community Tips & Tricks session, Christoph Neumüller and Andi Grabner demonstrate dtctl — a fast, open‑source command‑line tool for managing Dynatrace resources (dashboards, workflows, settings, notebooks, DQL queries, and more). Watch live demos showing how to browse and edit objects from the CLI, run queries, automate workflow lifecycle (update → execute → debug → closed‑loop tests), and let AI agents generate and iterate configs.

Watch this video to learn:
- How to install and authenticate dtctl
- Browse & edit Dynatrace objects via CLI
- Run DQL queries directly from dtctl
- Automate and debug Dynatrace workflows
- How AI coding agents can consume dtctl to generate, fix, and evolve configurations

🔗 Useful links
GitHub Repo: https://dt-url.net/github-dtctl
Get started Guide: https://dt-url.net/dtctl-docs
Dynatrace MCP Server: https://dt-url.net/mcp

---------------------------—

title: Dynatrace Live Debugging for OpenTelemetry & Infra‑Only Environments
description:
Dynatrace Live Debugging is no longer limited to Full‑Stack OneAgent deployments. In this Observability Clinic, Andi and Josh show how to enable Live Debugging in Discovery and Infrastructure Mode — perfect for teams adopting OpenTelemetry or lightweight OneAgent setups. This guide covers the exact prerequisites, feature flags, OneAgent settings, IAM controls, and host‑level toggles required to safely enable code module injection in Discovery Mode. You’ll see a full step‑by‑step demo of enabling Live Debugging on a Java Spring Boot service running in a cloud VM with OneAgent in Discovery Mode — including setting breakpoints, capturing snapshots, and tracing distributed requests without pausing production services. Ideal for SREs, developers, OpenTelemetry adopters, and Dynatrace admins seeking deep code‑level visibility with minimal agent footprint.

🔗 Useful links
Watch the first Live Debugger video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k5UHTd2VfU0&t=353s
Dive into Live Debugger: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/observe/application-observability/live-debugger

title: Troubleshooting Web & Mobile Frontends: Dynatrace Experience Vitals in Action
description:
In this Observability Lab, Andi is joined by Bernat to explore the newest Dynatrace® Apps designed to help web and mobile developers understand, troubleshoot, and optimize frontend performance and errors across web and mobile.
Learn how the Experience Vitals app gives you a cross‑frontend Watchtower view, how to deep‑dive into performance issues, and how the Error Inspector streamlines debugging — including full session context, backend trace correlation, and mobile symbolicated stack traces.

title: Infrastructure and Operations for SREs
description:
Explore how Site Reliability Engineers (SREs), IT Operations, and infrastructure teams can leverage Dynatrace’s Infrastructure and Operations App to quickly detect, analyze, and remediate infrastructure-related issues.

Join Andreas and Michal as they walk through real-world use cases, including:
- How to start your day with the Problems App
- Investigating CPU saturation and deployment changes
- Using contribution charts for root cause analysis
- Exploring new features like process lists, container views, and network device monitoring
- Leveraging AI-powered problem explanations and troubleshooting guides

Whether you manage hosts, containers, or network devices, this video shows how Dynatrace connects infrastructure observability with application observability for faster resolution and improved resilience.

title: Scaling Dynatrace Access Control with Terraform | Permissions as Code
description:
In this second part of our IAM series, Andreas Grabner and Jon Ujkani explore how to scale Dynatrace access control horizontally using Terraform and permissions-as-code practices. Learn why manual ClickOps doesn’t scale, how to avoid permission drift, and how to embed IAM into your CI/CD workflows for enterprise-grade observability.

Topics Covered:
- Why scaling IAM matters for large organizations
- Benefits of treating permissions as code
- Dynatrace Terraform provider overview
- Live demo: Declarative IAM with Terraform
- Best practices for GitOps workflows and automation

title: Dynatrace Access Control Fundamentals | IAM Policies, Boundaries & Best Practices
description:
In this Observability Lab, Andreas Grabner and Florian Aigner dive deep into Identity and Access Management (IAM) for observability in Dynatrace. Learn why access control matters, explore attribute-based access control (ABAC) vs. role-based access control (RBAC), and see how policies, boundaries, and groups enable fine-grained permissions for data and platform features.

We’ll cover:
- Why access control is critical for compliance and security
- Dynatrace’s IAM pillars: Groups, Policies, Boundaries
- Best practices for onboarding and scaling IAM
- A live demo showing how to configure access for admins, business teams, and developers
- Advanced topics like time-based access and privacy controls

Whether you’re starting with Dynatrace or looking to refine your observability strategy, this session gives you the fundamentals to secure and scale access effectively.

🔗 Useful links
Advanced ABAC: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/manage/identity-access-management/permission-management/manage-user-permissions-policies/advanced/iam-global-conditions
IAM Limits: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/manage/identity-access-management/iam-limits
Upgrade to ABAC: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/manage/identity-access-management/permission-management/manage-user-permissions-policies/advanced/migrate-roles

title: How to secure your Grail Data with Attribute-Based Access Control│ABAC
description:
Discover how to leverage Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) in Dynatrace to ensure users only access the Grail data they need—nothing more, nothing less. In this video, we’ll walk you through how ABAC works for data access permissions, its benefits for secure and efficient access management, and how it empowers organizations to enforce granular permissions.
You’ll learn how to:
- Select which existing Grail fields should determine user groups and dictate Grail data access.
- Provide precise access to specific Grail datasets in Dynatrace.
- Enhance security and streamline operations with ABAC.

Whether you're an admin or responsible for data governance, this video will help you master ABAC and optimize your Grail data access strategy. Secure your data, simplify access, and boost efficiency today!

title: Master JSON Parsing with DQL Functions | jsonPath & jsonField
description:
Learn how to efficiently parse JSON logs in Dynatrace using the latest DQL functions: jsonPath and jsonField. 

In this session, Vikas (Senior Software Engineer at Dynatrace) walks through practical examples, advanced techniques, and best practices for extracting structured data from logs. Whether you’re dealing with simple JSON or complex nested structures, this tutorial will help you unlock powerful insights with minimal effort.

title: Universal Tips for Building Actionable Observability Dashboards
description:
Building dashboards is easy, but building dashboards that deliver information quickly, tell a story, and are understood and trusted by everyone is not. Whether building dashboards with business insights or for technical troubleshooting, there are some universal rules that allow everyone to create better, actionable dashboards than they have in the past!
In this tips and tricks session, we invited Aleksandra Kunert, Solutions Engineer at Dynatrace, who has extensive experience in data visualization. In her current role, she helps organizations deliver information gathered through observability in a more effective way. Tune in and learn from Aleksandra about how to structure your dashboards (from top left to bottom right or vice versa), provide context and scope of the data displayed, use headers to explain the timeframe of charts, explain terminology that not everyone understands, and leverage the power of donut and honeycomb charts.

📖 Chapters 📖
---------------------------—
00:00 - Introduction
00:31 - Aleksandra Kunert
01:02 - Good vs Not Good Dashboards
02:00 - Example dashboard

06:29 - Donut Charts
06:55 - Tile-specific timeframe
07:45 - Explain the importance of data
08:45 - Honeycombs

12:09 - Summary of Tips
-----------------------------

title: How to monitor n8n with Dynatrace│AI Observability
description:
n8n is an open source workflow automation tool designed for flexibility and ease of use. It’s quickly become an invaluable tool for developers and businesses thanks to its no-code interface and extensive integration options.  

Monitoring n8n's performance is essential to ensure workflows run smoothly, especially in environments where automation is critical. Tracking metrics such as execution times, error rates, and resource usage helps ensure that n8n continues to meet the demands of modern, automated processes. 

This video offers a hands-on, practical demonstration of how to utilize Dynatrace to thoroughly monitor the n8n platform and the workflows running on it.

title: Create a Burn Down Chart in Dynatrace
description:
Whether you track the number of story points in a sprint, the failure rate of a service over the course of a month, or the burn rate of your SLO (Service Level Objective) based Error Budget. One way to view this data is in the form of a Burn Down chart, which indicates how quickly you're completing your work items or the number of failures you allow before violating a contract.
In this session, Andi (Andreas) Grabner, DevRel at Dynatrace, shows you the three steps to chart any metric as a Burn Down in a Dynatrace Dashboard using the DQL functions arrayCumulativeSum and iCollectArray.

title: AI A/B Testing, AWS Bedrock, AI Compliance, and Security | What's New in Dynatrace
description:
Discover the latest Dynatrace updates designed to enhance AI monitoring, streamline compliance, and enrich security insights. Key highlights include A/B testing for AI models with Dynatrace, reference instrumentation patterns for AWS Bedrock AgentCore, Google Agent Developer Kit, and OpenAI’s agent SDK, and tools to help comply with AI regulations like the EU’s Artificial Intelligence Act using AWS EventBridge and OpenTelemetry. Additionally, learn how to enrich security data from tools like Snyk, SonarQube, and Amazon GuardDuty with runtime contextual information, and discover how preformatted lookup tables can simplify telemetry enrichment. title: End-2-End Business Observability for SAP with Dynatrace
description:
Systems from SAP power many business processes of large enterprises worldwide. SAP is often a black box, though, when it comes to understanding and optimizing end-to-end business processes that span multiple systems that connect to it

To fulfill the Dynatrace promise of "understanding your business like never before" for enterprises relying on SAP Systems, we have invited Daniele Pfingsttage from SoftwareOne. 

He will show us how PowerConnect provides observability into SAP S/4HANA, ECC, PI/PO, BW, and how this data connects with all other observability data in Dynatrace to proactively detect end-to-end business impact, performance, security, or infrastructure issues!

📖 Chapters 📖
---------------------------—
00:00 - Introduction
00:51 - What is PowerConnect
02:48 - Dynatrace PowerConnect App
03:57 - SAP Basis Health Dashboard
05:30 - SAP Security Essentials - Risk Assessment
06:50 - SAP Background Jobs Status
08:13 - IDOC to Business Object Relationship
08:50 - PI/PO to ABAP SXMB Monitoring
10:11 - Summary of PowerConnect Use Cases
12:25 - PowerConnect Architecture
16:16 - Wrap Up
-----------------------------

title: Dynatrace + VCARB | Raffaele Boschetti
description:
Raffaele Boschetti, Head of IT at Visa Cash App Racing Bulls, faces a unique challenge: extracting crucial insights from massive amounts of data while his team works in silos across various car components. 

Here’s where the magic happens. With Dynatrace's observability platform, his team can: 

🔎 Mine valuable insights from complex data streams 
📈 Transform raw data into actionable intelligence 
💡 Identify exactly where improvements need to happen 

The result? Better performance on the track and clearer direction for the entire team. 

Sometimes the biggest breakthroughs come from seeing what was always there—you just need the right tools to uncover it.

title: Integrating Dynatrace DQL into IntelliJ for Engineers
description:
Whether you're maintaining DQL (Dynatrace Query Language) queries that are part of your Monitoring and Infra as Code repository, whether you're building your own apps or scripts that execute DQLs, or if you're creating Dynatrace Apps where you execute DQLs. If you're doing your development work in IntelliJ, then this Dynatrace DQL Plugin for JetBrains IntelliJ will automatically give you
- Code Completion and Formatting
- Syntax Highlighting
- Validation Checks
- and the option to execute DQLs and get the results in your IDE

In this video, Dorian Krefft walks us through the plugin he built, open-sourced, and made available on GitHub and the IntelliJ Marketplace.

📖 Chapters 📖
---------------------------—
00:00 - Introduction
00:40 - Why we built this plugin
01:25 - Automatic Code Completion
01:54 - Execute DQLs and analyze results
03:12 - Reformat all DQL in your repo
03:50 - DQL Features in other languages
04:47 - Install the plugin
05:40 - Plugin configurations
08:03 - Wrap-up and final thoughts
-----------------------------

title: Ingesting SonarQube Data into Dynatrace
description:
Security is a key software quality requirement. Combining security insights from tools like SonarQube with performance, functional, and resiliency observability data in Dynatrace allows security teams to get a comprehensive overview of the state of security in your software development process.
To learn more about the new SonarQube Extension, we invited Johannes Oezkan-Preisinger, Product Security Engineer at Dynatrace, who built this integration initially for internal use before the Dynatrace Extension team brought it to the Hub.
Johannes walks us through the metrics, logs, scan events, and SDLC events that the extension ingests. He shows us how to use the ready-made dashboards and how to build customized dashboards on this data. He reveals some tips and tricks on how this data can be used by security and quality teams and explains how to easily configure ingesting data from one or many SonarQube instances.


📖 Chapters 📖
---------------------------—
00:00 - Introduction
00:55 - Why integrate SonarQube and Dynatrace 
02:01 - Customized SonarQube Dashboards
04:37 - Vulnerability findings dashboard
05:12 - Security product coverage dashboard
05:44 - How to configure the SonarQube Extension
09:17 - Wrap Up
-----------------------------

title: Optimize Your Logs | Save Money and Boost Performance
description:
In this video we are going to discuss the new best practices guide, based on feedback from our pre-sales teams, D1 organization and product teams.

Christian Schwendemann, Principal Product Manager at Dynatrace is walking us through the different best practices and explaining how new and existing customers can optimize for query performance and reduce cost at the same time.

Christian explains:
- Bucket & query strategy basics
- Positive impact of using filters in your query
- Introduces the option "Retain with Included Queries"
- Walks us through benefits of Segments and how to create them
- Explains IAM (identity and access management) policies with field level permissions
- Compares Policy Boundaries to IAM Policies and when to use them (ABAC - Attribute Based Access Control)
- Showcases how to limit novice users to access only "Retain with Included Queries" using IAM policies

🔗 Useful links
Learn more about best practices for Log Management: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/analyze-explore-automate/logs/lma-best-practices
Explore Dynatrace with ready-to-use log telemetry: https://www.dynatrace.com/signup/playground/
Dynatrace University: https://university.dynatrace.com
Try out Dynatrace with a free trial: https://dt-url.net/trial

---------------------------—

title: Identify and Troubleshoot Business Problems in your Apps
description:
Businesses run on Software, and Software produces more observability data than ever before. Yet most organization are not extracting real-time business data from their real-time observability data. Instead, many organizations build up a business observability silo that slows down teams when troubleshooting business-impacting problems.

In this video, Andreas (Andi) Grabner, CNCF Ambassador and DevRel at Dynatrace, walks us through a reference observability implementation in Dynatrace for the OpenTelemetry Demo App AstroShop to show how to extract and connect real-time business insights with technical root cause information. 

Tune in and learn how to
- Extract and Analyze Business Metrics
- Drill from a Business Impact to the Technical Root Cause
- Hand over the Problem to your engineers
- Identify the problematic Line of Code to fix the problem fast

📖 Chapters 📖
---------------------------—
00:00 - Introduction
00:58 - Use Case Overview
01:15 - Extracting Business Insights
01:33 - Analyzing Real Time Business KPIs
02:41 - Drill to Technical Root Cause
03:26 - Hand-Off to Engineering
05:05 - Troubleshooting for future problems
06:04 - Wrap Up
-----------------------------

title: Enrich Observability Data with Lookup Tables in Dynatrace | FreedomPay Demo
description:
Discover how storing lookup data in Dynatrace Grail helps you enrich your observability pipeline with custom business context. In this Tips & Tricks session, Daniel Adams from FreedomPay shares a real-world use case showing how developers can transform raw trace data filled with cryptic IDs into human-readable insights.

✅ Learn how FreedomPay:

1️⃣ Uses lookup tables to make dashboards more meaningful for end users
2️⃣ Ingests lookup data from SQL databases, CSVs, and JSON files
3️⃣ Leverages the new DQL load and lookup commands to join business events, logs, spans, and metrics with custom data

Whether you're working with complex trace IDs or trying to bring clarity to your observability data, this session will show you how to make your data actionable and readable.

📖 Chapters 📖
---------------------------
00:00 - Introduction
00:43 - Lookup data: What problems it solves
02:06 - Lookup data doc page
02:45 - Sample CSV File
03:22 - Upload File to Grail
05:25 - Explore Lookup data using load
06:24 - Connect BizEvents with Lookup data
07:59 - Enrich Dashboards
10:03 - Lookup data in Dashboard variables
12:12 - Wrap Up
-----------------------------

title: Self-Service Observability for Cloud-Native Apps
description:
Thanks to Cloud Native, Observability data is growing exponentially. To deliver value from all this data, we must provide self-service experiences for engineers to get their actionable answers without having to be experts in analyzing logs, metrics, and traces from OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, or OneAgent.

In this video, Andreas (Andi) Grabner, CNCF Ambassador and DevRel at Dynatrace, walks us through a reference observability implementation in Dynatrace for the OpenTelemetry Demo App AstroShop. Andi will walk through self-service use cases showing how Davis CoPilot can be used to:
- Explain the root cause and remediation action of an ArgoCD Sync-related problem
- Pinpoint a representative trace for a high service failure rate issue with a single click
- Explain an exception that caused a transaction to fail and how to fix it
- Explain the details of a cryptic log message and provide suggestions on what to do next
- Get an overview of the 4 Golden Signals across all services

📖 Chapters 📖
---------------------------—
00:00 - Introduction
01:51 - Service Failure Analysis
02:30 - Trace Details of Failure
03:10 - Davis Detected Problems
03:38 - Problem Analysis
04:30 - Explain Problem with Davis CoPilot
05:10 - ArgoCD Sync Context on Problem
05:34 - Analyze Failure from Problem
06:34 - Troubleshooting Guides
07:57 - Explain a problematic Trace
09:38 - Explain error logs
11:12 - 4 Golden Signals
11:56 - Wrap Up

title: How to use OpenPipeline to redirect data to custom buckets
description:
In this video, learn how to redirect and store data in Dynatrace using custom buckets to define retention periods. Whether you're dealing with logs, metrics, business events, or spans, this guide shows you how to:

- Understand how data flows into Dynatrace and is stored in buckets via OpenPipeline®.
- Create custom buckets with specific retention periods (for example, up to 10 years).
- Redirect incoming data to these buckets using pipelines and dynamic routes.
- Verify that your data is stored correctly without changing your ingest scripts.

This tutorial is perfect for anyone looking to optimize data storage and retention in Dynatrace based on their requirements.

🔧 Topics Covered:
- Storage Management in Dynatrace
- OpenPipeline® Configuration
- Creating and Redirecting Data to Custom Buckets
- Setting Up Dynamic Routes

🔗 Useful links
Learn more about OpenPipeline in our docs: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/discover-dynatrace/platform/openpipeline

title: Ensure service health with Dynatrace | Dynatrace App Spotlights
description:
If you're an SRE (Site Reliability Engineer) responsible for managing the 4 Golden Signals of hundreds or thousands of services, or if you're part of a Development Team that needs quick access to your logs, metrics, and traces to identify problems, this video is made for you!

Whether you're ingesting your observability data via OpenTelemetry or the OneAgent, the Dynatrace Services app revolutionizes application service observability, offering real-time monitoring of service health and performance across hybrid cloud environments.
In this Spotlight Episode, Andreas (Andi) Grabner walks you through the relevant use cases the Service app provides to identify hotspots and explains how to get started!

title: Find the Unknown in Logs, Metrics, and Traces | Observability Forensics
description:
When disaster strikes: How to find the information you need when you don't know what to look for? Which logs and traces happened at the same time period to get more context? What was the performance of the underlying infrastructure connected to those logs and traces? 
As somebody on call to investigate problems, it's important to be efficient when responding to incidents. In this episode, Tiit Hallas, Principal Product Manager at Dynatrace, walks us through some tips and, tricks, and best practices for finding the unknown by breaking data silos for log analysis and incident response with the Dynatrace Security Investigator! 

Tiit will show:
- When to use the Security Navigator vs Notebooks and Dashboards
- How to find time-based correlated logs more easily with the new reference timeframe feature
- How to pivot on any log attribute to refocus your investigation up, down, left, and right the stack
- How to get instant access to performance metrics of the infrastructure, the logs, and traces that came from
- And how to easily switch between logs, metrics, and traces without losing context

🔗 Useful links
Explore the Security Investigator on our Dynatrace Playground: https://dt-url.net/j50315m 
Read the blog on Metrics Based Investigation: https://www.dynatrace.com/news/blog/enhanced-incident-response-based-on-performance-metric-insights/
Learn more about IP based Investigation: https://www.dynatrace.com/news/blog/security-investigator-offers-reputation-analysis-and-context-for-ip-addresses/
And check out the blog on Investigation Pivot: https://www.dynatrace.com/news/blog/pivot-the-perspective-of-your-investigative-queries-with-security-investigator/

title: Analyze Incidents with Davis® AI | Dynatrace App Spotlights
description:
Problems provides AI-assisted problem analysis optimized for DevOps, Operations, and SREs. When a large-scale incident occurs, Davis® AI identifies the root cause, pinpoints all relevant details, and visually reproduces the incident in charts, highlighting the affected deployment.

In this spotlight video, Andi (Andreas) Grabner walks you through how to use Problems efficiently. You'll learn how Dynatrace AI can explain a single problem or the commonality across multiple problems, as well as "Follow the red" to quickly identify the problematic distributed trace, critical log, problematic metric, or root-cause change. 
Andi also shows how you can enrich problems with your own metadata, such as ownership information, and how you can leverage the new Troubleshooting Guide integration to surface previous findings of similar problems to the team that takes action on an active problem.

🔗 Useful links

title: Enhance your Dashboards | Prompt Engineering with Davis CoPilot®
description:
Dynatrace Generative AI is deeply embedded in the Dynatrace Observability platform. Everything from "Explain the root cause of this problem and suggested remediation steps" to "Create a dashboard with the top error log patterns" is possible out of the box. As every organization is different, there is a need to create custom dashboards that visualize your relevant data. To make this data explainable for everyone, Dynatrace allows you to enrich your dashboards with customized domain-specific prompts for Davis CoPilot®

To learn how to enrich your dashboards with domain-specific prompts, we have invited Thomas Brandl, senior principal Solutions Engineer at Dynatrace, to this Tips and Tricks session. Thomas walks us through the details of creating prompts, enriching them with more context, and making them actionable in any of your custom dashboards. As a bonus, Thomas shows a new Workflow Action for Davis CoPilot®, which is currently in preview, to automatically generate the results of those prompts!

title: AI Observability explained | Gain insight into your AI models and agents
description:
The top reasons why AI projects fail are: bad performance of prompts, exploding costs to run the models and agents, unacceptable prompt output quality, and challenges to stay compliant!

According to Accenture's AI Investment Report, 84% of executives believe they need AI to grow business impact and improve efficiency. However, according to McKinsey, only 33% of all AI applications currently make it to production. By the end of 2027, Gartner predicts that 40% of AI projects will also be canceled. This is a lot of lost opportunity, money, and competitive advantage.

AI Observability is the practice of tackling those problems as it provides automated, real-time insights into AI systems. The data allows developers, data scientists, and operations to optimize their models, prompts, agentic algorithms, and deployments to run faster, more cost-efficiently, while also staying compliant

In this video, Andreas (Andi) Grabner, CNCF Ambassador and DevRel at Dynatrace, walks you through some of the best practices for using Dynatrace for AI Observability. Andi will also show you the out-of-the-box experience for AI Observability on the Dynatrace Playground, which is free for you to learn and follow!

🔗 Useful links
Visit the AI Observability Launchpad: https://dt-url.net/devrel-llm-launchpad-playground
Dive deeper into AI Observability: https://www.dynatrace.com/knowledge-base/ai-observability/
Sources: https://www.accenture.com/us-en/insights/artificial-intelligence/ai-investments

title: Kubernetes Autoscaling Based on Custom Metrics
description:
In this episode, we’re diving deep into Kubernetes autoscaling—but with a twist.

🚀 What You’ll Learn:
• The difference between HPA and VPA, and when to use each
• Why default CPU/memory metrics can be misleading
• How to use external metrics (like traffic volume) for more accurate scaling
• How to correlate resource usage with application health using OpenTelemetry and Dynatrace
• The magic of Pearson correlation to identify real scaling opportunities
• How to automate scaling decisions with DQL, EdgeConnect, and GitOps workflows

🔧 Tools & Concepts Covered:
• Kubernetes HPA & VPA
• Dynatrace Operator & DQL
• OpenTelemetry Collector
• KEDA & KEPN
• EdgeConnect for secure cluster interaction
• GitOps-friendly scaling policies

📈 Whether you're running microservices at scale or just getting started with Kubernetes, this episode will help you build a data-driven autoscaling strategy that actually works.

👍 Like what you see?
Don’t forget to like, subscribe, and hit the bell so you never miss an episode of Observe & Resolve!

Business Observability is about getting real-time business insights from your existing investment in foundational observability (logs, metrics, traces, real user data, etc.) to connect and analyze business and technical insights in context!

title: Get Real-Time Business Insights with Dynatrace
description:
Business Observability is getting real-time business insights from your existing investment in foundational observability (logs, metrics, traces, real user data, etc.). Dynatrace provides that capability and helps our customers improve their business outcomes, reduce costs, increase collaboration between IT and Business, and automate the remediation of problems that impact the business!
In this session, see Business Observability in Action with Klaus Enzenhofer, Principal Product Manager at Dynatrace. He walks us through real-life examples on the Dynatrace Playground. title: Integrate Observability into Your Dev Workflow
description:
"Bring actionable insights from your observability data TO your engineers and DON'T ask them to learn yet another tool to find answers themselves!"

If you struggle to prove the value of observability to your engineers and leadership, check out the following video. Andreas Grabner, CNCF Ambassador and Dynatrace DevRel, walks you through several simple tips and tricks for integrating Dynatrace into your existing processes and tools. 

In Part 1, you'll learn how to provide actionable answers from your observability into your IDP's Service Catalogue (e.g, Backstage, Port, Cortex, Kratix, Humanitec) or into your Pull Request (GitHub, GitLab, ...). Then you'll learn how to leverage Dynatrace Launchpads to provide a customized Dynatrace experience for engineers to show them what they need, vs overwhelming them with too much information.

title: Where’s My Build? Unlocking SDLC Insights with Dynatrace
description:
SDLC (Software Development Lifecycle) Observability tracks every artifact from development to production, following its lifecycle through all your DevOps tools and pipelines. This helps engineering leads answer questions like: Where is my build? Why is my pipeline so slow? Which tests have been executed, and which ones block the release to production?
In this video, you'll learn about how to implement SDLC Observability using Dynatrace SDLC Ingest from tools such as ArgoCD, Flux, Jenkins, GitHub, GitLab, or Azure DevOps. You can also expand this to your testing tools, security tools, or release automation tools.

🔗 Useful links
SDLC Getting Started Notebook: https://dt-url.net/devrel-pg-nb-sdlc-gettingstarted
Pipeline Observability Doc: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/deliver/pipeline-observability-sdlc-events
GitHub repo with configurations: https://github.com/Dynatrace/dynatrace-configuration-as-code-samples/tree/main
Blog about SDLC Observability: https://www.dynatrace.com/news/blog/observability-throughout-the-software-development-lifecycle/

title: How to Detect Bad Patterns in Logs and Traces
description:
Inefficient Database Queries (N+1), Excessive Logging per Request, Problematic Exceptions, or CPU Hotspots of your internal methods. Those are very common problem patterns we can identify if we connect and analyze Logs and Traces from your distributed applications and services. In this video, Andi (Andreas) Grabner, DevRel at Dynatrace, walks you through a couple of Tips and Tricks on how to identify those patterns using Dynatrace Distributed Traces and Logs as well as some more advanced use cases using DQL (Dynatrace Query Language) to join Traces and Logs!

title: eBPF Monitoring with Inspektor Gadget and Dynatrace
description:
Discover how eBPF is revolutionizing Kubernetes observability and security and learn how to overcome its performance challenges in this deep dive episode of Observe & Resolve.

If you’re exploring eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) and its potential in Kubernetes environments, this video is a must-watch. Our experts break down complex concepts to guide you through monitoring and optimizing eBPF resource usage effectively.

Here’s what you’ll get:
- A clear explanation of eBPF and its role in kernel-level observability.
- Insights into why Kubernetes’ kubelet might fail to spot critical resource issues.
- Practical demonstrations of using Inspektor Gadget, OpenPipeline, and the Dynatrace Operator for efficient monitoring.
- Two powerful solutions for better observability:
1. Bpfstats for streamlined metric collection.
2. Top eBPF (bpftop) for granular, program-level insights using logs.

We’ll also show you how to set up observability pipelines, transform metrics, and create dashboards to visualize CPU and memory usage of eBPF probes. By the end of the video, you’ll have actionable tools and strategies to fine-tune your cluster’s performance while maintaining top-tier security.


🔗 Useful links : 
Check out Inspektor Gadget: https://inspektor-gadget.io/
Visit the repository related to this video on GitHub: https://dt-url.net/5i03005

title: Create real-time visualizations from K8s data | Dynatrace App Spotlights
description:
Dashboards allows you to get answers out of any of your observability data that you send to Dynatrace. Whether it's your logs, metrics, traces, business events, real user behavior, synthetics, security, or compliance data, Dashboards is the place to build powerful, real-time, and interactive visualizations.
In this spotlight video, Andi (Andreas) Grabner walks you through creating a new dashboard from scratch. The example he chose focuses on metrics and logs from a Kubernetes environment. Andi walks through creating charts, tables, and variables to make the dashboard dynamic and reusable.

title: Transform Data Analysis with Microsoft Power BI and Dynatrace Grail
description:
Empower your reporting with the combined strengths of Dynatrace and Microsoft Power BI.

Join Imran Khan as he introduces the powerful Dynatrace Microsoft Power BI Connector for Grail. Learn how to effortlessly integrate any data from Grail into your Microsoft Power BI reports and dashboards. Whether you're working with Power BI Semantic Models, Dataflows, or Fabric (Dataflow Gen2), discover how this integration unlocks richer, more dynamic reporting.

Discover how Dynatrace Grail, the ultimate Data Lakehouse, seamlessly connects and stores all your observability data—from technical metrics like logs and traces to business insights like SLOs and user behavior.

Highlights include:
- Using Dynatrace Query Language (DQL) to transform your data
- Bringing AI-driven insights into Power BI
- Exploring use cases like security monitoring, deployment overviews, log analytics, and more

title: How to Set Up Live Debugging with Dynatrace: Step-by-Step Guide
description:
Dynatrace Live Debugger is a cloud-native and live data collection application that gives you instant access to the code-level data you need to troubleshoot and debug quickly in any environment, from dev to production. With a single click, access the necessary and relevant data without adding additional code, waiting for redeployment, or attempting to reproduce issues locally. Additionally, using non-breaking breakpoints allows you to instantly see the complete state of your app, including stack trace, variable values, and more, all without stopping or breaking your running code.
In this video, Josh Hendriks, Technology Strategist at Dynatrace, walks us through all the necessary setup and configuration steps to enable Dynatrace Live Debugging in your environment.

title: TELUS maximizes revenue during peak sales moments with Dynatrace
description:
TELUS is one of Canada’s largest telecommunications companies, providing a wide range of world-class technology solutions to businesses and consumers. To drive its continued growth, TELUS underwent a transformation to modernize its IT operating model. It embraced a new, more dynamic application architecture and migrated services from on-premises infrastructure to the cloud. This transformation was underpinned by an observability strategy that enabled a more proactive approach to managing customer experiences.

While this approach improved the performance and resilience of its applications, TELUS saw an opportunity to extend its observability-driven insights across the business. The company has a complex sales ecosystem, consisting of systems used by internal teams and third-party retail partners. TELUS wanted to extend its use of observability data to visualize what customers were experiencing across any sales channel in real time. This is particularly important during sales events like Black Friday, when customers will opt to shop elsewhere if the experience isn’t perfect. Read the story, here: https://dynatr.ac/4dbhN1D

title: How to Analyze GitHub Health Using OpenTelemetry with Dynatrace
description:
How healthy are your GitHub Workflows, Jobs, or Actions across all your GitHub repositories? If actions fail, do they fail across all workflows and repositories? What are the top-consuming repositories, branches, triggers, and actors? Where do they spend their time, and how can it be optimized?

Philipp Lengauer, senior principal Product Manager at Dynatrace, had the same questions for his personal GitHub projects. So, he created a new Open-Source project that can automatically instrument Bash, GitHub Actions, and Workflows with OpenTelemetry. In this Community Tips and Tricks session, Philipp analyzes the OpenTelemetry logs, metrics, and traces to analyze GitHub Health.
In a follow-up video, we dive deeper into the technical implementation and how everyone can leverage his open source project for their GitHub Health Observability: https://youtu.be/GP7HIPA5rFE

title: How to Instrument GitHub Workflows and Actions Using OpenTelemetry
description:
While GitHub provides Webhooks to get some level of observability into pull requests, jobs, and workflows, details about what slows workflows down or what actions are taking place in detail and why they may fail are limited! 
Philipp Lengauer, Sr Principal Product Manager at Dynatrace, needed to understand where his jobs, actions, and workflows slow down or fail. To get that level of detail, he created a new open source project that can automatically instrument Bash, GitHub Actions, and Workflows with OpenTelemetry. 
In this Community Tips & Tricks session, Philipp walks us through enabling OpenTelemetry instrumentation for GitHub repositories and how to then analyze the resulting OpenTelemetry logs, metrics, and traces to optimize your GitHub workflow, action and job usage, and health!
If you want to see what else you can do with this data, make sure to watch the second video we recorded, where we focused on GitHub Health Analytics: https://youtu.be/4EVFj3fJwy8

title: Turn Off Unnecessary Workloads: Kube-green & Dynatrace
description:
This episode explores how to turn off unnecessary workloads in non-production environments using the CNCF project Kube-green. It builds on our previous discussion about measuring energy usage with Kepler and takes it further by automating the process to make your workloads greener.

In this video, you'll learn:
• Why turning off unuseful workloads is crucial for sustainability.
• How to set up Kubegreen to manage sleep and wake-up times for namespaces.
• Configuring Istio to produce access logs in OpenTelemetry format.
• Deploying the Dynatrace Distro for OpenTelemetry to create a metric pipeline for Envoy logs and Kubegreen metrics.
• Automating the process with Dynatrace workflows to optimize energy usage.

Here's what we'll cover:
1. Setting up Kube-green.
2. Configuring Istio for access logs.
3. Deploying Dynatrace OpenTelemetry and operator.
4. Creating a DQL query to report first and last requests.
5. Building a Dynatrace workflow to automate Kube-green configurations.
 
🔗 Useful links
Learn more about kube-green: https://kube-green.dev
Visit the Dynatrace Playground tenant: https://dt-url.net/obs-resolve-kubegreen
GitHub tutorial: https://dt-url.net/obs-resolve-kubegreen-github
Watch the previous video about Kepler: https://youtu.be/3YmXzg3zQAU

title: How to do Network Observability with Dynatrace
description:
Applications and services wouldn't be able to communicate without a robust, resilient and healthy network. In this observability lab we have invited Lucas Hocker, Product Lead for Infrastructure Observability at Dynatrace, to see how to setup automatic monitoring of your routers, switches and other network devices with Dynatrace's support for protocols such as SNMP, Syslog, Traps, Netflow or Pings.

Tune in and learn how to leverage auto-discovery of your network component and, with that, easily expand your network observability to ensure your applications can run on a robust, resilient, and healthy network infrastructure!

title: Live Debugging of the OpenTelemetry Demo App with Dynatrace
description:
What if you could debug your OpenTelemetry logs, metrics, and traces directly in your favorite IDE without impacting your running application? This video guides you to leveraging Dynatrace Live Debugger to achieve just that. 

Combining OpenTelemetry's rich observability data with Dynatrace powerful debugging capabilities, you'll learn how to streamline issue resolution and eliminate roadblocks in your production environment.

Join Josh Hendricks, Technology Strategist at Dynatrace, as he demonstrates live debugging of the OpenTelemetry Demo app. Here's what you'll discover:
- Access runtime variables, stack traces, and code profiling within your IDE (Visual Studio Code, IntelliJ).
- A walkthrough of integrating OpenTelemetry (logs, metrics, and traces) with Dynatrace for a seamless debugging experience.
- Why Dynatrace’s live debugging enhances efficiency and empowers developers to manage their production more efficiently.

This solution will show you how to gain deeper visibility, take control of runtime insights, and resolve issues faster than ever.

title: Simplify Your Dev Workflow with Live Debugger
description:
Unlock the next level of productivity with Dynatrace Live Debugger! This powerful tool allows developers to troubleshoot live bugs in production environments without the hassle of traditional logging methods. Whether you're using Visual Studio Code or IntelliJ, Dynatrace provides a smooth and intuitive experience, helping you pinpoint issues faster and keep your applications running seamlessly.

Join Josh Hendricks, Technology Strategist at Dynatrace, as he demonstrates how developers can leverage Live Debugging to troubleshoot live bugs in real time by:
- Connecting your IDE (Visual Studio Code, IntelliJ) to a Dynatrace-observed environment  
- Setting non-breaking breakpoints without impacting runtime behavior  
- Analyzing stack traces and variables for every execution of your code  
- Resolving production issues efficiently with the same convenience as debugging in local environments  

Plus, you'll get a front-row seat to a live demo, showing Live Debugger functionalities and breaking down complex troubleshooting tasks into simple, actionable steps.  

🔗 Useful links

title: Real User Monitoring on Microsoft Power Platform
description:
Discover how real-time insights can revolutionize your application performance! This video breaks down Kupp Software's Dynatrace integration and how it enhances client-side application monitoring within the Microsoft Power Platform. Whether you’re managing Model-Driven Apps or tackling Case Management scenarios, this solution comprehensively monitors user interactions and optimizes the user experience.  

Here's what you'll learn in this video:
- Real User Monitoring (RUM): See how the Dynatrace RUM agent captures detailed user interaction data to understand how users engage with your app.
Session Replay: Watch user sessions to pinpoint navigation patterns and uncover pain points, helping you resolve issues proactively.  
- Performance Optimization: Learn how to identify bottlenecks and resolve client-side issues before they affect users.  
- Practical Use Cases: Robert and Andi walk you through real-world scenarios, showcasing the power of this integration for businesses.  

Gain actionable insights into performance bottlenecks, identify issues before they impact users, and optimize your applications for maximum efficiency. With real-time user interaction monitoring, you can track how users engage with your app, pinpoint areas for improvement, and enhance overall functionality. This ensures your applications deliver a smooth, seamless experience that keeps users satisfied and engaged. Whether you're an app owner striving to retain users or a performance engineer focused on delivering top-tier functionality, these tools provide the data and insights you need to stay ahead of the competition and meet user expectations.

title: Enhancing Security and Compliance with Dynatrace: A Guide for SREs
description:
Keeping a system resilient also includes detecting, prioritizing, and remediating security and compliance findings that violate DORA, NIST, CIS, or STIG. Dynatrace Security Posture Management provides Cloud Native SREs and Security and Compliance Engineers continuous insights for remediating security posture and compliance findings, including misconfigurations and regulatory compliance assessments.
In this video, Michiel de Lepper, Product Manager at Dynatrace, explains how Dynatrace allows you to stay compliant. You'll learn that the compliance data—stored in Grail—is not limited to the new Security Posture Management App. Learn how to do custom reporting and automation and integrate this with your SRE processes and tools.

title: Optimize Your Microsoft Teams Experience with Real-Time Insights
description:
Curious about how to streamline Microsoft Teams performance monitoring and issue resolution? 

This in-depth session features Julien Egron from Phenisys, winners of the Dynatrace partner awards, and creators of the custom-built Dynatrace® App that ensures automated observability and analytics for Microsoft Teams. Designed to empower helpdesk and operations teams, the app makes performance management smarter and more efficient with accessible telemetry data and scalable analytics.  

What you'll learn in this video:  
- How the MS Teams Observability Cockpit centralizes insights into Teams performance.  
- An overview of MS Teams sites and their performance analytics.  
- Advanced diagnostic tools for troubleshooting calls and identifying issues quickly.  
- Insights into Microsoft Issues Feed for real-time problem detection.  
- The app's architecture and how it delivers actionable, real-time insights.  
- Best practices dashboards that guide teams in managing MS Teams performance effectively.  

This video will help you unlock the full capabilities of Teams monitoring. You’ll leave equipped with tools and strategies to take your organization's collaboration efficiency to the next level!

title: Reduce Energy Consumption in Your Kubernetes Cluster
description:
In this episode, we're tackling one of the most crucial topics in the cloud-native world:  Sustainability. 

☁️ Observe & Resolve – Cloud-Native Troubleshooting Made Simple!

Join Henrik Rexed as he explores how to measure and optimize energy usage in your Kubernetes environment using Dynatrace OpenPipeline and Kepler. With the rapid adoption of cloud technology and the rise of AI, data centers are consuming more energy than ever. It's time to take action and reduce our carbon footprint! 

In this video, you'll learn: 
- Why sustainability is essential in the cloud-native world. 
- How to measure energy consumption with Kepler. 
- Steps to optimize your workloads and reduce energy usage. 
- How to use Dynatrace OpenPipeline to transform and visualize Kepler metrics. 
- Building a dashboard to track energy savings and efficiency. 

Here's what we'll cover: 
- Setting up Kepler using Helm. 
- Deploying the Dynatrace Distro for OpenTelemetry. 
- Creating a metric pipeline to collect and transform Kepler metrics. 
- Building and analyzing a dashboard to visualize energy usage. 

Try It Out: If you'd like to try this in your environment, check out the GitHub repository for all the resources and instructions. 

🔗 Useful links
Access additional resources on the Observe & Resolve GitHub: https://dt-url.net/ab03wy6
Learn more about Kepler: https://sustainable-computing.io/

title: Unleash the Power of Distributed Traces
description:
Distributed Traces have empowered Architects, SREs, and Performance Engineers to understand and optimize complex distributed systems. Thanks to OpenTelemetry, Distributed Traces are now also available in cloud-native environments. But what is a span, and how is it different from a request or a trace? How would one analyze traces, and what advanced analytics use cases are possible when combining traces with logs, metrics, or topology data?

In this episode, Christoph Neumueller, senior principal Product Architect at Dynatrace, walks us through the anatomy of a trace and how Dynatrace unleashes its power by connecting all signals of observability and making them queryable through DQL (Dynatrace Query Language).

🔗 Useful links
Visit the Traces Notebook on the Dynatrace Playground: https://dt-url.net/devrel-pg-traces-usecase-notebook

title: Dynatrace OpenPipeline: The Ultimate Tool for Istio/Envoy Log Management
description:
This episode dives into Istio log management and showcases how Dynatrace OpenPipeline can be a game-changer for developers working with Istio and Envoy.  

We start by introducing you to the fundamentals of Istio, explaining why it’s a top choice for Kubernetes clusters and the role Envoy logs play in improving observability.  

Here’s a glimpse of what you’ll learn in this episode:
- Why logs are essential for observability and how OpenTelemetry can standardize them.  
- A breakdown of Envoy logs in Istio’s sidecar mode and the insights they provide.  
- How to transform logs into actionable metrics using Dynatrace OpenPipeline.  
- Step-by-step guidance on configuring Istio, setting up log agents, deploying the Dynatrace operator, and more.  
- Designing a DQL query to extract key insights and filtering fields to streamline your logs.  
- Tips to configure a new Envoy pipeline to generate metrics and minimize log storage footprint.  

Henrik will walk you through these technical processes so you can focus on leveraging Istio and Dynatrace OpenPipeline to their fullest potential. By the end of the video, you’ll have a clear roadmap for improving efficiency while reducing the complexity of your observability setup.  

☁️ Observe & Resolve – Cloud-Native Troubleshooting Made Simple!

🔗 Useful links
Access additional resources on the Observe & Resolve GitHub: https://dt-url.net/cq23wzh
Watch my other video about Istio: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mOxF4cdBTd8
Learn more about Istio OpenTelemetry logs on their official documentation: https://istio.io/latest/docs/tasks/observability/logs/otel-provider/
Read more about Envoy Access logs: https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/configuration/observability/access_log/usage

- a breakdown of envoy logs in istio’s sidecar mode and the insights they provide.  
- how to transform logs into actionable metrics using dynatrace openpipeline.  
- step-by-step guidance on configuring istio, setting up log agents, deploying the dynatrace operator, and more.  
- designing a dql query to extract key insights and filtering fields to streamline your logs.  
- tips to configure a new envoy pipeline to generate metrics and minimize log storage footprint.  

henrik will walk you through these technical processes so you can focus on leveraging istio and dynatrace openpipeline to their fullest potential. by the end of the video, you’ll have a clear roadmap for improving efficiency while reducing the complexity of your observability setup.  

☁️ observe & resolve – cloud-native troubleshooting made simple!

title: Trace-Based Pattern Detection in Distributed Systems
description:
Chatty services, excessive database queries, frequent exceptions, and similar patterns are some of the most common issues in distributed architectures. These issues often compromise the performance and resiliency of applications and services.

Distributed Traces give us insights into a distributed application and make it easy to spot those patterns before they become a problem.

In this episode, Josef Mayrhofer, Founder of Performetriks, shares a Dynatrace dashboard he uses to analyze and optimize distributed applications in load-testing and production environments.

title: How to Baseline and Forecast with Dynatrace
description:
This tutorial will show you how to spot traffic spikes and loads using seasonal and auto-adaptive baselining. You'll learn to forecast future trends, which will help you anticipate when your system will hit its limits or achieve your business goals.

Whether you're sending logs, metrics, traces, or events to Dynatrace. You can do so much more than putting this data on a dashboard! 

Dynatrace Davis® AI can use any time series (from metrics, logs, traces, events...) and provide additional insights and trigger actions such as:
Alert on abnormal behavior, eg: an unusual spike in traffic
Forecast system behavior, eg: are we staying within our error budgets
Execute predictive automation, eg: scale our disks to prevent loss of data

This video is Part 1 of a series on harnessing any type of observability data for effective baselining and forecasting. Stay tuned for Part 2, where you'll discover how to use this data for predictive automation, like right-sizing your environments before issues arise.

🔗 Useful links
View the Dynatrace Notebook on Playground: https://dt-url.net/devrel-notebook-pg-howto-forecast-predict-jan2025

title: OpenPipeline Overview: Enrich Logs, Extract Metrics, Create Events
description:
What is OpenPipeline? OpenPipeline is the Dynatrace data handling solution for seamlessly ingesting and processing data from different sources at any scale and format in the Dynatrace Platform.

In this session, learn how OpenPipeline works, how it fits into the Dynatrace architecture, and the value it provides. You'll also gain an understanding of the core concepts, such as ingest data sources, routing rules, and pipeline steps. Thomas Billicsich, Sr. Product Manager for OpenPipeline, will give us a quick overview of Dynatrace OpenPipeline, how it works, how it fits into the Dynatrace architecture, the value it provides, and explains the concepts of using OpenPipeline to enrich logs, extract metrics, create events and store them in Grail.

title: Dynatrace Synthetic for DevOps and SREs: New Features Explained
description:
Dynatrace Synthetic has helped DevOps, SREs, Platform Engineers, and Developers validate the health and availability of critical services, endpoints, and user journeys. Synthetic has a new Dynatrace App that brings more capabilities around Analytics and Network Availability Monitoring (NAM) and makes Synthetic Metrics, Results, and Events available in Notebooks, Dashboards, and Workflows. 

In this session, the product team walks us through Synthetic's use cases and demos the new capabilities available for all Dynatrace SaaS users as of January 2025.

title: Automate Release Validation with Observability-Based Scorecarding
description:
Scorecarding is a common software development concept used to validate an artifact's quality or deployment across many dimensions. 
The Dynatrace Site Reliability Guardian (SRG) allows you to score your deployments across all the layers of your stack by analyzing logs, metrics, traces, events, security, and more.

In this Tips & Tricks session, you'll learn about best practice SRG configuration and how to automate those checks in your software delivery process.

title: Quick Start: Making Logs Actionable with Dynatrace
description:
Logs are a great source for troubleshooting issues, detecting bad patterns, proactive alerting, or usage analytics. To leverage logs effectively, we need to ensure that they're properly ingested and connected to the rest of your observability data (traces, metrics, events, etc.).
Not everyone is an expert in those best practices or analyzing logs to identify problems or patterns, so we have built a quick-start package to make your logs actionable without having to be an expert.
In this video, you'll see how to explore this quick-start package in the Dynatrace Playground Tenant and how you can use it in your environment to optimize the usage of your logs

title: Cloud Cost Transparency with Dynatrace fine grained Cost Allocation
description:
Due to rapid innovation, the #Dynatrace platform is now utilized across enterprise departments and is invaluable beyond central IT teams. The new, fine-grained Dynatrace Cost Allocation feature enables the automated attribution of Dynatrace costs to your departments, teams, or apps. This significantly reduces overhead and provides new cost transparency and control, extending the existing cost management features of the most customer-friendly licensing model available in the observability market.

title: Air France-KLM delivers seamless, sustainable flying experiences with Dynatrace
description:
In this video, Jos Kerssens, Senior Vice President of IT Operations & Cloud Transformation, and MaiLan Tran, Team Manager of Monitoring, share how Air France-KLM uses #Dynatrace to optimize IT operations to provide seamless and sustainable flying experiences for its passengers. 

With end-to-end observability and cloud readiness, the airline can quickly identify and resolve issues, reducing operational and revenue impact. With 80% of its e-commerce applications now on Azure and data analytics on GCP, Air France-KLM supports its sustainability goals while delivering exceptional customer experiences. 

Hear how the insights from Dynatrace enable proactive problem-solving, supporting continuous improvement and reinvestment strategies.

title: Release Validation for DevOps Engineers with Site Reliability Guardian
description:
Explore how to ensure the stability of microservices and manage feature releases using #Dynatrace. 

Key Highlights:
Learn how to monitor service health and assess the impact of new feature releases.
Understand how to use Dynatrace auto-baselining capabilities to set dynamic performance thresholds.
Discover how to create a Site Reliability Guardian to maintain application reliability.
See how to evaluate feature flags in a development environment and make data-driven "go / no go" decisions.

🔗 Useful links
Explore our sample workflow and SRG on the Dynatrace Playground: https://dt-url.net/release-validation-workflow and https://dt-url.net/release-validation-srg
Get hands-on and deploy our release validation with the SRG demo app using GitHub Codespace: https://dt-url.net/obslab-release-validation
Implement release validation with SRG in your environments with our detailed Dynatrace Documentation: https://dt-url.net/release-validation-docs

title: Composite Site Reliability Guardians: Ensuring Service and Application Level Health
description:
Modern applications are no longer monolithic but are composites of many different services. The health of each service can be validated through Service Level Objectives (SLOs) such as Latency, Failure Rate, Traffic, and Saturation using a #Dynatrace Site Reliability Guardian (SRG). 

In this session, we learn from Stan Switalski, Sr. Solutions Engineer at Dynatrace, on how to leverage Composite SRGs to observe and alert on Application Level Health across all depending services.

🔗 Useful links
Access additional resources on GitHub: https://dynatr.ac/4eBdSuI
Learn more about the Site Reliability Guardian in our Dynatrace documentation: https://dynatr.ac/4dIkLZL
Try out Dynatrace with a free trial: https://dt-url.net/trial

title: GitLab Pipelines Observability with OpenTelemetry and Dynatrace
description:
Modern Delivery Pipelines are quickly becoming very complex due to the amount of tools and technologies they orchestrate. As organizations grow pipelines not only grow in size but also various versions need to be maintained. Taming this complexity requires central observability of all pipeline executions including metrics, traces and logs.

In this session, Nicholas Wörner, a DevOps engineer at #Clario, and Andreas Grabner, a DevRel at #Dynatrace, demonstrate how OpenTelemetry provides the necessary pipeline insights and how Dynatrace provides the pipeline analytics on top!

Explore how you can leverage Dynatrace’s Davis® AI to forecast resource needs and automate pod scaling with workflows.

title: Self-Service Log Driven Problem Detection
description:
Learn how #Dynatrace can help you detect critical problems based on logs and streamline troubleshooting by enriching them with further data.

In this tutorial, Adam Gardner and Andreas Grabner demonstrate the end-to-end self-service use case based on the OpenTelemetry Demo app. Dynatrace will detect critical logs and notify teams automatically, enriching the problem description with additional relevant metadata for troubleshooting, such as important logs, traces, events, and a link to a Runbook. 

This video is for all development and application teams that custom develop or own packaged applications to automate incident detection and response based on critical logs generated by those components in any environment.

Get hands-on and deploy the app on GitHub Codespace: https://dynatr.ac/4emA6Au
