title: SQL Query Optimization in Kubernetes: Using PostgreSQL Logs for Analytics
description:
Explore how to collect and analyze SQL statements from your PostgreSQL database, including execution durations. Logs, analytics, and dashboards to gain deep insights into your database performance.

Here’s what you’ll learn: 
• Why Logs Matter: Configure PostgreSQL to log detailed SQL statements and execution times, focusing on slow queries. 
• Setting up Observability: Use Dynatrace Operator, Fluent Bit, and Dynatrace collector to capture logs and traces from your Kubernetes environment. 
• Analyzing SQL Statements: Visualize PostgreSQL logs and perform analytics to track query performance. 
• Dashboards and Workflow Automations: Turn SQL insights into real-time dashboards and trigger workflows based on slow queries.

Key Tools: 
• PostgreSQL logs for query insights 
• Dynatrace Operator for observability 
• Fluent Bit for log collection 
• Dynatrace collector for performance tracing

title: How to Diagnose Database Connection Pool Issues
description:
Connection Pools are critical components in distributed architectures as they allow services to connect to downstream systems such as a database or other microservice. Connection Pools need to be properly sized to not become a performance bottleneck under increased load.

In this session, we quickly walk you through analyzing a performance bottleneck of a service by looking into key metrics, traces, and database query executions of a distributed application on Kubernetes.

Getting Started with Dynatrace: https://github.com/dynatrace-perfclinics/dynatrace-getting-started
Watch the Dynatrace Database App Spotlight: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ec5nhhnVODI&list=PLqt2rd0eew1aHqfD-XRNVaIAiN-QxkPK6&index=5&pp=iAQB

📖 CHAPTERS 📖

title: Microsoft Power Platform Code Level Diagnostics through OpenTelemetry and Dynatrace
description:
As observability has evolved, achieving deep insights within SaaS offerings like #Microsoft Power Platform, a versatile suite for creating custom apps and automating business workflows, is still challenging. If you're struggling with performance issues or errors in your custom Power Platform plugins, this session with Robert Proell, Power Platform Architect at Kupp, is essential viewing.

Robert demonstrates their approach to providing automated observability for any custom code running in Power Platform without requiring code changes. Emitting OpenTelemetry traces allows these insights to be seamlessly analyzed in Dynatrace and other observability data, offering powerful code-level diagnostics. This session is particularly valuable for those involved in DevOps, SRE, and workflow automation.

Kupp Process Insights (KPI) is a comprehensive solution designed to monitor, analyze, and understand system architectures within the Azure, Power Platform, and Dynamics 365 CE ecosystems. It leverages OpenTelemetry (OTel) to provide telemetry data that integrates smoothly with existing Dynatrace deployments, enabling a unified view across your systems. KPI is tailored for stakeholders, including project managers, system architects, and developers. It ensures project compliance, aids in making well-informed decisions, and provides detailed insights into code-based and non-code components. KPI supports documentation generation and report planning and prioritizes security and privacy, making it indispensable for maintaining and optimizing system performance in Microsoft PowerApps and Azure environments.

Link to Hub: https://dynatr.ac/3MmhhB9

title: Log Pattern Analysis with Dynatrace
description:
Too many useless logs, new errors, or a spike in business-relevant logs after a deployment or configuration change are log patterns you need to be aware of.

To give you a head start, Thomas Brandl, Principal Solutions Engineer at #Dynatrace, is sharing his Log Pattern Analysis Dashboard with us, where he automatically detects common patterns across all logs while following common best practices such as focusing the analysis on Dynatrace buckets, filtering on namespaces or log level or using filters based on keywords.

Whether you ingest your logs through Fluent, OpenTelemetry, OneAgent or any other log data source, the dashboard from Thomas can be used for all your log data.

Access the dashboard on the Playground Tenant: https://dynatr.ac/4csoslS

title: Business Flow - August 2024 Update - Dynatrace App Spotlight
description:
Business Flow enables IT teams and business owners to monitor and analyze critical business processes end-to-end, ensuring seamless business process management (BPM) and business process optimization. This powerful tool is essential for enhancing process management and driving continuous process improvement within your organization.

In this Dynatrace App Spotlight, we're back with Klaus Enzenhofer, Principal Product Manager at Dynatrace, who will walk us through the latest version of the Business Flow App. This update is designed to strengthen your cloud observability, providing deeper insights into application monitoring, application performance monitoring (APM), and cloud application security.

Whether you're focused on cloud monitoring, infrastructure monitoring, or Kubernetes monitoring, the latest enhancements in Business Flow offer robust capabilities for monitoring and optimizing business process improvement efforts. title: What's New in Dynatrace - v1.295
description:
In this episode, Berkan Akbulut and Milos Pejic cover the updates from #Dynatrace release 1.295. View the full release details here: https://dt-url.net/fm23qwk

Here are the most important updates:
1️⃣ New DQL Operator: Enhanced DQL queries with the new iIndex operator.
2️⃣ Dashboards and Notebooks: Improved menu entries, search functionality, and keyboard shortcuts. Added a new Business Events option for Business Analytics.
3️⃣ Log Ingestion API: Now supports all JSON data types for new custom processing pipelines.
4️⃣ Maintenance Window Page: Pre-selects the local browser time zone for better user experience.
5️⃣ Technology Overview Page: Displays all technologies for a process group, not just the main one.
6️⃣ Kubernetes Container Properties: Shows container image digest if available.
7️⃣ Security Update: Apache Commons Compress updated to address security vulnerabilities.
8️⃣ Distributed Traces: Added support for the CICS path name request attribute source.
9️⃣ OpenPipeline: Configure log, event, and business event processing pipelines with OpenPipeline.

A few resources were mentioned in this video. See the links below. 

To access the webinar at Dynatrace University, select https://www.dynatrace.com/dynatrace-university (Sign in to Dynatrace University. Select "On Demand." Select "Webinar Series" and click on the "Dynatrace Webinars Course." Select this video!)

Notebook v295 - Playground environment: https://dt-url.net/gv03qmk 
Dynatrace SaaS trial: https://bit.ly/dtsaastrial
OneAgent tutorials: https://bit.ly/oneagenttutorials
Dynatrace documentation: https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs
Dynatrace blogs: https://dynatr.ac/3Szj2PC
Dynatrace Community: https://dynatr.ac/3ugrKIl
If you have questions or feedback, don’t hesitate to reach out to the Dynatrace ONE team:
https://support.dynatrace.com/

title: What's New in Dynatrace - v1.293
description:
In this episode, Berkan Akbulut and Milos Pejic cover the updates from #Dynatrace release 1.293. View the full release details here: https://dynatr.ac/3Sph6Zo.

Here are the most important updates:

1️⃣ Auto-adaptive Thresholds for AI-driven Quality Gating: The Site Reliability Guardian now automates the validation process for new software releases or changes, setting specific performance, availability, or security objectives.
2️⃣ Histogram Charts: Added to Notebooks and Dashboards for visualizing and analyzing distributions.
3️⃣ Zoom Functionality: Enhanced chart interactions within line/bar/area and histogram charts to allow zooming in and out.
4️⃣ Document Ownership Visibility: Hovering over the Shared icon of a shared document now shows the document’s owner.
5️⃣ Fix Recommendations from Snyk: Displayed for vulnerabilities based on the Snyk feed, available in various sections including API and Grail.
6️⃣ Settings Audit Events in Grail: Changes to settings are now available as Grail events.
7️⃣ Query for Davis Problems and Events: Introduced dt.davis.problems and dt.davis.events for querying problems and events.
8️⃣ Team Name in Ownership: In the get_owners action, you can now find your team name in the Team dropdown list.

A few resources were mentioned in this video. See the links below. 

To access the webinar at Dynatrace University, select https://dynatr.ac/3WjYwCR. (Sign in to Dynatrace University. Select "On Demand." Select "Webinar Series" and click on the "Dynatrace Webinars Course." Select this video!)

title: What's New in Dynatrace - 1.294
description:
In this episode, Berkan Akbulut and Milos Pejic cover the updates from #Dynatrace release 1.294. View the full release details here: https://dt-url.net/bc63qw7 

Here are the most important updates:

1️⃣ Privacy Spotlight: Introduced record-level hard deletion in Grail to comply with end-user deletion requests in line with privacy laws.
2️⃣ Session Replay Extension: A new extension available in the Chrome store enhances session playback by proxy loading resources.
3️⃣ Application Observability: Improved handling of long-running fetches of requests and traces to better utilize system resources.
4️⃣ DQL Functions: Added exists function and new aggregation functions for time series and makeTimeseries commands.
5️⃣ Search Fields in Record List Visualization: Added a search input to find and filter specific field names easily.
6️⃣ Show DQL Query: Now you can display the underlying DQL query in Explore - Logs and Explore - Metrics.
7️⃣ Enriched Software Component Information: New attributes for querying software component versions and file hashes for Java technology.

A few resources were mentioned in this video. See the links below. 
To access the webinar at Dynatrace University, select https://dynatr.ac/3Ab2fLu. (Sign in to Dynatrace University. Select "On Demand." Select "Webinar Series" and click on the "Dynatrace Webinars Course." Select this video!)

title: How to observe GraalVM Native Images with Dynatrace
description:
GraalVM Native Image offers high performance for Java applications by pre-compiling Java code into native images. However, traditional observability methods for Java don't work with these native images, limiting you to basic process-based performance metrics. 

In this episode, Ivan Gudak, Lead Product Engineer at #Dynatrace, explains how Dynatrace enables comprehensive observability, including end-to-end distributed tracing, for GraalVM Native Image applications in cloud-native, virtualized, containerized, and Kubernetes (K8s) environments.

Links discussed in this observability lab:
GraalVM Support on Dynatrace Hub: https://dynatr.ac/3LSIxXO
GraalVM Native Image Instrumentation: https://dynatr.ac/46CiZry
Quarkus Native Image Instrumentation: https://dynatr.ac/46xIGZY
Sample GitHub repository: https://github.com/ihudak/weekplanner
Build Pipeline Example: https://github.com/ihudak/weekplanner/blob/master/.github/workflows/graal-native-x86.yml

Chapter List:
00:00 - Introduction and Welcome 
01:04 - The Significance of GraalVM
02:54 - Kubernetes (K8s) Overprovisioning: Causes and Consequences
04:00 - Cost Analysis and Savings Potential
04:48 - The Challenge of Observability in Cloud-Native Environments
06:00 - Enhancing Observability with Dynatrace for Native Images
07:18 - Instrumenting Native Images with Dynatrace
10:42 - Summary and Key Takeaways

title: Getting Started with Dynatrace Pattern Language (DPL)
description:
Welcome to our comprehensive guide on getting started with #Dynatrace Pattern Language (DPL). In this hands-on video, Indermohan Singh will walk you through the basics of DPL, a powerful tool for matching and extracting data within Dynatrace. 

You'll learn how to: 
• Match and extract data
• Use matchers like INT, LD, and IPADDR
• Configure matchers to suit your needs
• Define character groups and quantifiers
• Apply lookaround

Resources: 
Dynatrace Pattern Language (Doc): https://dynatr.ac/3xZ7ptx 
Dynatrace Query Language (Doc): https://dynatr.ac/3S1UttO 
DPL Architect: https://dynatr.ac/3S1K8xV 
Practical Guide to DQL (Dynatrace Query Language): https://youtu.be/RAtId3P8C6I?si=2_lk2RW60KjSEBxv 
Learn DQL Playlist: https://dynatr.ac/learndql
